rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BENJAMIN YEE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/07/2022. Second continuance for same reason. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DECLARATION OF TRUST FOR SHEILA CORRY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Substitute Personal Representatives Jennifer Corry And Colleen Corry Kellison In Place Of Deceased Respondent Eamon Corry Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/02/2022. 1) Please file a memorandum of points and authorities explaining why a petition was filed when the Code of Civil Procedure 377.31 says, "on motion..." It appears this should have been filed as a motion on the law and motion court calendar. 2) Please file an actual memorandum of points and authorities in support of the petition. 3) Notice is not proper. Please give 30 day's notice to all parties not just the attorneys in the matter. (Probate Code 1202). 4) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. GREEN REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Title To Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/02/2022. Second continuance for same issues. 1. Venue is a threshold issue that must be determined prior to the petition. Please explain and provide the legal basis /cite probate code sections for why San Francisco is the proper venue when the trustee appears to live in Edgewood, MD. 2. Please file evidence showing the Wells Fargo bank account ending in 9287 was in existence prior to execution of the trust. 3. Please file a current account statement of the bank account. 4. Please explain all the Decedent's heirs so the court can determine whether notice is proper under Prob. C. 851 (b). 5. The proposed order does not match what is requested in the prayer/request in the petition. It appears the petition must be amended to match what is requested in the proposed order as the prayer/request in the petition does not indicate the account number or where the bank account is and just says "Directing that the Bank Account is an asset of the William H. Green Revocable Trust." Also the second page of the proposed order "That the parties shall execute all documents and perform such additional acts as may be necessary to carry out the above" is not in the petition's prayer/request. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 01/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KINUE Y. SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for the court to determine whether sufficient effort has been made to give notice to the decedent's brother and nephew, as the mailed notice to each was returned marked "unknown" but they were mailed to the same address as the decedent's sister, so it's not clear who marked them or why they were marked unknown. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Bridget Carter, Felicia Johnson, and Carl Williams, as other grandchildren of the decedent having equal priority for appointment as administrator, have not declined to serve as co-administrator or otherwise consented to the petitioner's appointment as sole administrator. 2) The petition requests full IAEA authority with the requirement of a blocked account in lieu of a bond. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.
